alerts.title: Israel-Lebanon border escalation; 17 killed in southern Lebanon strikes
alerts.summary: Israeli military conducted overnight strikes across southern Lebanon, killing at least 17 civilians according to BBC and Guardian reports (incidents feed, June 12, 03:28 UTC). Strikes targeted Tyre and surrounding areas, prompting evacuation orders. This represents active kinetic escalation on the northern front concurrent with Iran-U.S. de-escalation signals, raising risk of multi-front conflict.

alerts.title: Explosions detected in Sirik, Iran; anti-ship missiles reportedly launched toward Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: ME Spectator Telegram channel reports explosions in Sirik, southern Iran (June 11, 21:11 UTC, ~41K views), with analysis suggesting possible anti-ship missile or drone launches targeting the Strait of Hormuz. Alert follows Trump's announcement of de-escalation framework, indicating either delayed Iranian response or tactical probing of U.S. commitment.

alerts.title: U.S. maintains three carrier strike groups in active theater; Red Sea presence sustained
alerts.summary: Current assets data confirms USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) deployed across theater (assets feed, June 12, 03:28 UTC). Deployment pattern supports sustained force posture despite diplomatic pause, with three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) and amphibious assets (USS Bataan) reinforcing chokepoint control.
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf

alerts.title: IAEA demands Iran report full uranium stockpile as enrichment remains at 60% U-235
alerts.summary: UN watchdog continues demanding transparency on uranium stockpile amid Iran's maintenance of 60% enrichment with ~1-2 week breakout window (global_metrics, June 12, 03:28 UTC). IAEA board resolution underscores verification crisis: Iran's refusal to provide reporting compounds breakout-window risk despite kinetic pause.

alerts.title: U.S. domestic discourse remains polarized on Iran conflict; Trump dominance peaks at 102 mentions
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows U.S. opinion split 47% negative vs. 45% positive (sentiment feed, June 12, 03:28 UTC), with 'Trump' appearing 102 times in top-word analysis—up from 88 in previous cycle. Keyword emergence of 'clayton,' 'vance,' 'spacex' (combined 39 mentions) indicates White House personnel and policy focus now competing with Iran conflict narrative for attention.

analysis_note: U.S.-Iran kinetic exchange has paused following Trump's negotiation framework announcement, but tactical probes (Iran missile activity, Israeli Lebanon strikes) and nuclear enrichment persistence suggest underlying escalation risk remains acute; multi-theater activity and IAEA uranium reporting crisis compound strategic volatility.
